records.ety: [F. encombrer; pref. en- (L. in) + OF. combrer to hinder. See Cumber, and cf. Incumber.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Encumbered (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Encumbering.]

defs: To impede the motion or action of, as with a burden; to retard with something superfluous; to weigh down; to obstruct or embarrass; as, his movements were encumbered by his mantle; his mind is encumbered with useless learning.
quotes: Not encumbered with any notable inconvenience.

defs: To load with debts, or other legal claims; as, to encumber an estate with mortgages.
